name: Sector Physics Template
summary: Four defining parameters for each sector at E₁: self-coupling κ(S;d) as rational function of ι_τ, polarity signature (χ₊-dom/balanced/χ₋-dom/crossing), primorial depth d ∈ {1,2,3}, and kernel generator. Also defines PolaritySign inductive type.

name: EM Sector at E₁
summary: B-sector instantiation: γ-generator, self-coupling κ(B;2) = ι_τ², χ₊-dominant polarity, depth 2. Physical: photon transport, Maxwell equations, fine structure constant.

name: Strong Sector at E₁
summary: C-sector instantiation: η-generator, self-coupling κ(C;3) = ι_τ³/(1−ι_τ), χ₋-dominant polarity, depth 3. The (1−ι_τ) denominator is the structural signature of confinement.

name: Higgs Sector at E₁
summary: ω-sector instantiation: γ∩η crossing generator, self-coupling κ(B,C) = ι_τ³/(1+ι_τ), crossing polarity, depth 3. Unique +1 derived sector from the lemniscate crossing point. Physical: mass generation.

name: Gravity Sector at E₁
summary: D-sector instantiation: α-generator, self-coupling κ(D;1) = 1−ι_τ, χ₊-dominant polarity, depth 1. Temporal sector on base τ¹. Physical: frame holonomy, gravitational constant G = (c³/ℏ)·ι_τ².

name: Weak Sector at E₁
summary: A-sector instantiation: π-generator, self-coupling κ(A;1) = ι_τ, balanced polarity (unique), depth 1. The master constant ι_τ itself IS the weak self-coupling. Physical: temporal arrow, parity violation, beta decay.

name: Coupling Formula Map
summary: All 10 inter-sector couplings as rational functions of ι_τ. 4 self-couplings: κ(D)=1−ι_τ, κ(A)=ι_τ, κ(B)=ι_τ², κ(C)=ι_τ³/(1−ι_τ). 6 cross-couplings: electroweak, weak-strong, weak-gravity, EM-strong, EM-gravity, strong-gravity. Complete 10-entry coupling ledger.

name: Temporal Complement
summary: κ(A;1) + κ(D;1) = 1: the temporal pair (Weak + Gravity) exhausts the depth-1 coupling budget. Gravity and Weak are complements. Proof: ι_τ + (1−ι_τ) = 1.

name: Temporal Multiplicative Closure
summary: κ(A,D) = κ(A;1)·κ(D;1): the temporal cross-coupling is exactly the product of the two temporal self-couplings. The temporal pair is multiplicatively closed.

name: All Couplings Positive
summary: All 10 coupling numerators are strictly positive. Since all denominators are positive by construction, all coupling values are strictly positive. Physical: no sector is decoupled.

name: Spectral Fine Structure
summary: Spectral fine structure constant α ≈ (8/15)·ι_τ⁴. Leading-order approximation from two τ² surface modes (exponent 4) and primorial factor 8/15 = 2³/(3·5). Gives α ≈ 0.00725, 1/α ≈ 137.9 (spectral approx, ~0.06% from experiment).

name: α Numerical Range
summary: α_spectral is in the range (7200, 7400)/10⁶, bracketing the experimental value α ≈ 7297.35/10⁶. Equivalently, 1/α is between 135 and 139, bracketing the experimental 137.036.

name: Holonomy vs Spectral
summary: Two complementary α derivations: (1) Spectral: α ≈ (8/15)·ι_τ⁴ (closed-form approximation). (2) Holonomy: α = (π³/16)·Q⁴/(M²H³L⁶) (exact, requires calibration cascade from Book IV Part VII). Connection: α = (8/15)·ι_τ⁴·R(ι_τ) where R ≈ 1.0065.

name: Wrong Formula Correction
summary: The 1st Edition reference sheet formula α = (ι_τ/2)⁴ is INCORRECT. It gives α ≈ 0.000849 (off by factor ~8.5). The correct spectral formula is (8/15)·ι_τ⁴. Ratio: correct/wrong = 128/15 ≈ 8.533. Lean-verified refutation.

name: Power Hierarchy
summary: Power hierarchy: κ(B;2) = κ(A;1)² (EM = Weak squared) and κ(A,C) = κ(A;1)³ (Weak-Strong = Weak cubed). The coupling tower is a pure power series in the master constant ι_τ.

name: Primary Invariant

name: Carrier Type
summary: Carrier type for physical quantities in τ³ = τ¹ ×_f T²: Fiber (T², microcosm), Base (τ¹, macrocosm), or Crossing (lemniscate point).

name: Physical Quantity Template

name: Particle Kind

name: Planck Character

name: Uncertainty Product

summary: Coulomb constant: k_e = (π²/32) · Q²/(M·H·L³). Coefficient 1/32, π², exponents (-1,-3,-1,2). τ-derived k_e ≈ 8.987 × 10⁹ N·m²/C² (0.00% match).

name: Vacuum Permittivity

name: Vacuum Permeability
summary: Vacuum permeability: μ₀ = (π³/8) · Q²/(M·H³·L⁵). Coefficient 1/8, π³, exponents (-1,-5,-3,2). τ-derived μ₀ ≈ 1.257 × 10⁻⁶ H/m (0.00% match).

name: Maxwell Relation
summary: c² = 1/(ε₀·μ₀): proven algebraically from dimensional exponents and π-prefactors. Dimensional: ε₀+μ₀ exponents = -2×c exponents. Prefactors: (8/π³)·(π³/8) = 1. Pure structural identity, no numerical approximation.

name: Coulomb-Permittivity
summary: k_e = 1/(4π·ε₀): proven from dimensional exponent cancellation and π-prefactor arithmetic. k_e+ε₀ exponents = zero. Prefactors: (1/32)·4·(8) = 1, π^(2+1-3) = π⁰. Pure algebraic identity.

name: G Frontier

name: Epstein Zeta Structure
summary: Epstein zeta function Z(s; iι_τ) on rectangular lattice with shape parameter ι_τ. Structure records shape numer/denom, evaluation point s, scope label.

name: Chowla-Selberg Decomposition
summary: Chowla-Selberg decomposition: Z(s;iι_τ) = 2ζ(2s) + C(s)·ι_τ^(1-2s) + Bessel_sum. Three terms: constant (negligible), leading power-law, exponentially suppressed Bessel.

name: Leading Exponent
summary: Chowla-Selberg leading exponent at s=4 is -7 (= 1-2×4). Origin of ι_τ^(-7) in mass ratio formula. Proved by rfl.

name: Normalization
summary: N = Z(4)/R ≈ 5.935 is NOT algebraic. R is bulk-to-surface breathing ratio of Hodge Laplacian, not Z(4)/constant.

name: Lemniscate Three-Fold
summary: Three distinguished supports on L = S¹∨S¹: {Lobe₁, Lobe₂, Crossing}. Regular simplex in spectral metric with pairwise distance √3.

name: Spectral Distance √3
summary: √3 ≈ 17320508/10000000 (7 digits). Arises as |1-ω| where ω = e^{2πi/3}. Approximation quality: (√3_approx)² < 3 < (√3_approx)²·(1+10⁻⁷).

name: Three-Fold Distance Squared
summary: |1 - ω|² = 3 where ω = e^{2πi/3}. Proof: (3/2)² + (√3/2)² = 9/4 + 3/4 = 12/4 = 3. Origin of √3 in mass ratio.

name: √3 Approximation Quality
summary: 17320508² × 100000 > 299999 × 10000000²: the √3 rational approximation is accurate to 7+ significant digits. Proved by native_decide.

name: √3 Triad
summary: Same √3 in 3 formulas: (1) R₀ correction, (2) δ_A/m_n = (√3/2)·ι_τ⁶, (3) α_G = α¹⁸·√3·κ. R correction is tau-effective; δ_A and α_G conjectural.

name: Triple Holonomy
summary: Three independent U(1) circles in τ³ = τ¹ ×_f T²: base circle, fiber circle 1, fiber circle 2. Each contributes factor π through holonomy. Product: π³.

name: Holonomy Correction
summary: π³α² holonomy correction. π³ ≈ 31006277/1000000 from three circles. α² from spectral formula squared. Combined: ~0.00165 (three orders below √3).

name: Correction Smallness
summary: π³α² < 0.002 and > 0.001: the holonomy correction is real but 1000× smaller than √3. Perturbative hierarchy. Proved by simp.

name: Charge Conjugation
summary: C: α → −α kills first-order holonomy. Surviving order is α². This is why Level 1+ has π³α² not π³α.

name: Mass Ratio Bulk Term
summary: ι_τ^(-7) bulk term: numer = 10⁴², denom = 341304⁷. Range: (1846, 1850) at 6-digit approximation, ~1853.6 at exact ι_τ. Proved by native_decide.

name: Level 0 Formula
summary: R₀ = ι_τ^(-7) − √3·ι_τ^(-2). Level 0 mass ratio formula. Correction √3·ι_τ^(-2) ∈ (14, 16). At exact ι_τ: 7.7 ppm accuracy. At 6-digit approx: R₀ ∈ (1831, 1836).

name: Level 1+ Formula
summary: R₁ = ι_τ^(-7) − (√3+π³α²)·ι_τ^(-2). Level 1+ formula with holonomy correction. At exact ι_τ: 0.025 ppm accuracy. Structure records exponents and accuracy.

name: Bulk Overshoots
summary: ι_τ^(-7) > R_CODATA even at 6-digit precision (1847.7 > 1838.68). Correction must be subtracted. Proved by native_decide on 50-digit Nat comparison.

name: Level 0 Range
summary: R₀ ∈ (1831, 1836) at 6-digit approximation. Deviation from CODATA < 1%. Proved by native_decide on 60-digit Nat arithmetic avoiding subtraction.

name: Derivation Chain Complete
summary: ALL 10 links in the R derivation chain are tau-effective. Zero conjectural ingredients. 10/10 scope = tau-effective. Proved by native_decide on list filter.

name: All Links Tau-Effective
summary: 10 tau-effective links, 0 conjectural, 0 metaphorical. Scope count verified by native_decide. The R formula has ZERO conjectural ingredients.

name: CR-Manifold
summary: A CR-manifold of type (k, l) is a real smooth manifold of dimension 2k+l equipped with a rank-2k horizontal distribution H, a complex structure J on H with J^2 = -id, and an integrability condition on Lie brackets of (1,0)-sections.

name: Integrability Criterion
summary: A CR-structure (H, J) is integrable if and only if the Nijenhuis tensor N_J vanishes on H. Equivalent to Lie bracket closure of Gamma(H^{1,0}).

name: CR-Structure on τ³
summary: The CR-structure on τ³ is the triple (H, J, ν): H = fiber tangent T² (spanned by ∂/∂θ_γ, ∂/∂θ_η), J rotates within H (J² = -id), ν = base direction ∂/∂t. CR-type (1,1): one complex dimension, one real codimension.

name: Integrability of τ³ CR-Structure
summary: The CR-structure (H, J, ν) on τ³ is integrable. Follows from the torus fiber T² carrying a globally flat complex structure: coordinate vector fields commute and N_J vanishes identically on H.

name: Character Modes
summary: Character modes on fiber T² are χ_{m,n}(θ_γ, θ_η) = exp(i(mθ_γ + nθ_η)) for (m,n) ∈ Z². Mode label m counts γ-circle windings (EM lobe), n counts η-circle windings (Strong lobe).

name: CR-Address
summary: A CR-address is a pair (m,n) ∈ Z² labeling a character mode χ_{m,n} on fiber T². The full address lattice is Λ_full = Z².

name: Address Precision (ch16)
summary: Address precision P(f) = sup_{(m,n)} |f̂_{m,n}|² ∈ (0,1] for a normalized state f ∈ L²(T²). Maximal precision 1 iff f is a pure character mode.

name: Wedge Holonomy
summary: The holonomy of character mode χ_{m,n} around a closed loop encircling the wedge point of L is Hol(ℓ; m,n) = (-1)^{m+n}. Derived from opposite complex orientations of the two lobes at ω.

name: CR Parity Constraint
summary: A character mode χ_{m,n} is CR-admissible on τ³ iff m+n ≡ 0 mod 2. Only modes with even parity sum are compatible with the CR-structure.

name: CR-Admissible Sublattice
summary: The CR-admissible sublattice Λ_CR = {(m,n) ∈ Z² : m+n ≡ 0 mod 2}. Index-2 sublattice (checkerboard), generated by (1,1) and (1,-1).

name: Density Halving
summary: Λ_CR has density 1/2 in Z². The CR parity constraint eliminates exactly half the naive mode space.

name: Physical Factor of Two
summary: The density halving explains many factors of 2 or 1/2 in QM: spin-1/2, Pauli exclusion, Dirac factor 2, uncertainty 1/2. All from CR parity eliminating half the modes.

name: Emergence of Spin-1/2
summary: Bi-rotation on T² constrained by CR-parity produces the SU(2) double cover of SO(3). Pure fiber modes carry integer spin s = (m+n)/2; mixed base-fiber modes carry half-integer spin.

name: Character on a Space
summary: A character on a path-connected space X is a group homomorphism χ: π₁(X) → U(1). The set Char(X) := Hom(π₁(X), U(1)).

name: Characters on T²
summary: A character on T² is determined by (m,n) ∈ Z×Z via χ_{m,n}(θ_γ,θ_η) = exp(i(mθ_γ + nθ_η)). m = γ-winding (Radials), n = η-winding (Beacons).

name: Character Variety of T²
summary: Char(T²) := Hom(Z×Z, U(1)) ≅ U(1)×U(1) = T². The address book of quantum states is itself a torus — Pontryagin duality.

name: Automatic Quantization
summary: Admissible quantum addresses are restricted to Λ_CR by CR-compatibility, parity constraint, and ι_τ coupling lock. Discrete spectrum is automatic — derived from topology, not postulated.

name: Address Precision (ch17)
summary: Address precision δ(ψ, (m₀,n₀)) := |c_{m₀,n₀}|² ∈ [0,1]. When δ = 1, the state is perfectly resolved (eigenstate). Born rule is the Pythagorean theorem in H_τ.

name: Geometric Charge
summary: Geometric charge from relative orientation of two fundamental rotations (γ-tube and η-hole). Charge sign q = sgn(m·n_ref − n·m_ref) relative to a reference orientation.

name: Charge Quantization from Winding
summary: Electric charge is an integer multiple of e_τ from the minimal non-trivial character mode in Λ_CR. Charge quantization is automatic: winding numbers are integers.

name: Fractional Charges and Confinement
summary: Quark fractional charges (1/3, 2/3) correspond to a finer sublattice of Λ_CR accessible only within confined systems. Confinement denominator (1−ι_τ) never truncates: fractional-charge addresses are permanently unresolvable.

name: Energy Duality
summary: E = mc² (fiber stiffness) and E = ℏω (base frequency) are the same eigenvalue of H_∞ = ι_τ²·Δ_Hodge, viewed from fiber and base decompositions respectively.

name: Sharp and Spread States
summary: Sharp state: δ ~ 1 at one address (classical limit). Spread state: δ comparable at many addresses (genuinely quantum).

name: Conjugate Precision Trade-off
summary: Sharpening address precision in one direction spreads it in the conjugate: Δ_γ · Δ_{p_γ} ≥ ℏ_τ/2. Preview of the Address-Obstruction Theorem (ch20).

name: Quasi-Ergodic Coverage
summary: Bi-rotation on T² with angular-rate ratio ι_τ (transcendental) is quasi-ergodic: orbit dense, never repeating. Explains neutron substructurelessness, particle diversity, and decay via Diophantine resonance.

name: Space of CR-Functions
summary: CR(τ³) = {f: τ³ → C | ∂̄_b f = 0}. Physical states satisfying the holomorphic constraint coupling fiber T² and base τ¹.

name: Algebraic Properties of CR(τ³)
summary: CR(τ³) is a complex vector space, commutative algebra, and infinite-dimensional.

name: Canonical Inner Product
summary: ⟨f,g⟩ = ∫_{τ³} f̄·g dμ where dμ is the fibered product measure with μ(T²) = 1.

name: Inner Product Properties
summary: Sesquilinear, Hermitian, positive definite. Positive definiteness uses CR identity theorem.

name: Inner Product Uniqueness
summary: The canonical inner product is unique given σ-equivariance, ρ-compatibility, and fiber normalization.

name: Holomorphic Hilbert Space
summary: H_τ = L²-completion of CR(τ³). The quantum state space, obtained by completing the pre-Hilbert space of CR-functions.

name: Hilbert Space Properties
summary: H_τ is complete, separable, infinite-dimensional — the three von Neumann axiom properties.

name: Central Theorem Implies Boundary Determination
summary: Book II Central Theorem O(τ³) = A_spec(L) induces H_τ ≅ L²(L̂, dν_spec). The Hilbert space is determined by boundary spectral data.

name: Orthonormal Basis
summary: {χ_{m,n} : (m,n) ∈ Λ_CR} is an orthonormal basis for H_τ. Orthonormality from Haar; completeness from Peter-Weyl restricted to CR-sublattice.

name: Spectral Completeness
summary: Every f ∈ H_τ decomposes uniquely as f = Σ c_{m,n} χ_{m,n} with convergence in the H_τ norm.

name: Physical State Space
summary: Physical states satisfy normalization and stability (eigenstate or finite superposition of H_∞ eigenstates). H_phys ⊂ H_τ.

name: Entanglement
summary: A two-particle state is entangled if its coefficient tensor on H_τ⊗H_τ does not factor. Entanglement = non-factorizability of CR-functions on T²×T².

name: Generic Entanglement
summary: Separable states have measure zero; entangled states are dense and generic. Rank-1 tensors have infinite codimension.

name: Superposition from Linearity
summary: If f, g ∈ H_τ then αf + βg ∈ H_τ. Superposition is a theorem about linearity of ∂̄_b, not a postulate.

name: Holomorphic Vector Field
summary: A vector field X on τ³ is holomorphic if [X, ∂̄_b] = 0. The Lie algebra X_CR(τ³) of holomorphic vector fields.

name: Quantum Operator
summary: X̂f(p) = d/dt|_{t=0} f(φ_t(p)) for holomorphic vector field X. Defined by geometric flow — no quantization rule invoked.

name: Commutator Equals Lifted Lie Bracket
summary: [X̂, Ŷ] = [X,Y]^: operator commutator equals the operator of the Lie bracket. Bridges geometry and algebra.

name: Topological Quantization
summary: Compact T² → discrete Z² → Λ_CR → all quantum operator spectra are discrete subsets of ℏ_τ·Z. Quantization from topology, not postulated.

name: Canonical Commutation Relation
summary: [X̂, P̂] = iℏ_τ. Derived from CR-coupling; ℏ_τ = 1/4 in τ-units from bi-rotation normalization. A theorem, not a postulate.

name: Observable
summary: Self-adjoint operator Â: H_τ → H_τ with ⟨f, Âg⟩ = ⟨Âf, g⟩. Guarantees real eigenvalues, orthogonal eigenstates, spectral completeness.

name: X and P Are Self-Adjoint
summary: X̂ = -iℏ_τ ∂/∂θ_γ and P̂ = -iℏ_τ ∂/∂θ_η are self-adjoint. Boundary terms vanish because T² is compact.

name: Address Uncertainty
summary: Position uncertainty (Δx)² and momentum uncertainty (Δp)² for a normalized state ψ ∈ H_τ, defined as expectation-value variances of X-hat and P-hat. Quantifies address precision on the fiber T².

name: τ-Normal Form for Joint Address
summary: A τ-normal form for joint address precision is a state ψ ∈ H_τ that simultaneously minimizes the uncertainty product U(ψ) = Δx·Δp. The infimal state of the joint-address optimization problem.

name: Phase Transport Witness
summary: The phase transport witness W_ω(f) at the lemniscate crossing point ω measures the rate at which γ-address and η-address information diverge. Defined as the derivative of the difference f|_{S¹_γ} − f|_{S¹_η} at ω.

name: Clopen Position Localization
summary: A state f ∈ H_τ is clopenly localized in position at scale ε if its γ-address distribution is supported on a clopen subset U_γ ⊂ S¹_γ of angular measure |U_γ| ≤ ε. Natural localization notion on compact profinite fiber.

name: Phase Transport Monotonicity
summary: For a normalized state clopenly localized at scale ε: (Δp)_f ≥ ℏ_τ/(2ε). Monotone: finer localization forces strictly larger momentum uncertainty. First link of the three-theorem chain.

name: No-Joint-Minimum Theorem
summary: No state ψ ∈ H_τ achieves U(ψ) = 0. For all normalized ψ: Δx·Δp ≥ ℏ_τ/2. The γ- and η-address lattices are CR-coupled, and no single τ-normal form minimizes uncertainty in both simultaneously.

name: σ-Equivariant Crossing-Point Mediator
summary: A σ-equivariant crossing-point mediator h > 0 on L = S¹∨S¹ is a constant arising from a character χ ∈ L-hat at ω, satisfying σ-equivariance (χ∘σ = χ) and geometric mean consistency h² = h_γ·h_η.

name: Planck Character Uniqueness
summary: ℏ_τ is the UNIQUE σ-equivariant crossing-point mediator on L. Among all σ-equivariant characters χ_{k,k}, exactly one yields a positive mediator: χ_{1,1}|_L with value ℏ_τ. Third link of the three-theorem chain.

name: Canonical Saturation State
summary: ψ_sat = (1/√2)(χ_{1,1} + χ_{-1,-1}) = √2·cos(θ_γ + θ_η): the real-valued, σ-invariant superposition of the lowest non-trivial σ-equivariant modes.

name: Saturation Equality
summary: The canonical saturation state achieves exact equality: (Δx)_{ψ_sat}·(Δp)_{ψ_sat} = ℏ_τ/2. The Heisenberg bound is sharp, attained by the σ-fixed character at the crossing point.

name: Heisenberg Uncertainty (Position-Momentum)
summary: For all normalized ψ ∈ H_τ: Δx·Δp ≥ ℏ_τ/2. The bound is sharp (attained by ψ_sat), unique (ℏ_τ is the only mediator), and structural (CR-coupling on T² ⊂ τ³, not measurement limitation).

name: Heisenberg Uncertainty (Time-Energy)
summary: For all states on τ³: Δt·ΔE ≥ ℏ_τ/2. Same CR-coupling mechanism as x-p case: base τ¹ (time) couples to fiber T² (energy) through the same structure.

name: Existence Not Measurement
summary: The address obstruction is ontological, not epistemological. A state with Δx = 0 and Δp = 0 does not merely resist observation; it fails to be a legitimate element of H_τ.

name: Role of ℏ_τ in Obstruction
summary: The bound ℏ_τ/2 is the minimum non-zero value of the uncertainty product, set by geometric coupling between address directions. Non-vanishing ℏ_τ IS the structural content of QM.

name: Determinism and (×,∧)-Tension
summary: Deterministic CR-function evolution and apparent measurement randomness manifest Book III's (×,∧)-tension. The universe is deterministic through ×; it appears random through ∧.

name: Address Resolution
summary: Measurement of an observable on ψ ∈ H_τ is address resolution: coupling ψ to a device whose pointer has a definite eigenvalue, selecting a single eigenstate. No collapse axiom.

name: Born Rule
summary: P(m₀,n₀) = |c_{m₀,n₀}|²: probability of address resolution yielding (m₀,n₀). A theorem from Pythagorean geometry (Parseval) + Gleason uniqueness on infinite-dimensional H_τ. Not a postulate.

name: Measurement Repeatability
summary: After address resolution yields outcome a_k, the post-resolution state is ψ_k. Immediate second measurement yields a_k with certainty: P(a_k) = 1.

name: Decoherence
summary: Suppression of off-diagonal elements of the reduced density matrix due to entanglement with environmental degrees of freedom. Converts quantum superposition into classical mixture.

name: Classical Limit
summary: In the limit |m|,|n| → ∞ with mℏ_τ → x and nℏ_τ → p continuous, expectation values satisfy classical equations of motion (Ehrenfest).

name: Schrödinger Equation
summary: iℏ_τ ∂ψ/∂t = H_∞ ψ, where H_∞ = ι_τ² Δ_Hodge. Derived from holomorphic flow along the base τ¹ via Stone's theorem + Cayley graph analysis. A theorem, not a postulate.

name: Determinism-Probability Reconciliation
summary: Substrate determinism and outcome probability are simultaneously true: they describe different levels of description. The full state determines the distribution but not the individual outcome.

name: Measurement Problem Dissolves
summary: The measurement problem dissolves in τ³: measurement is a consequence of address structure. Multi-address state + single-address device = single outcome. No interpretation needed.

name: Zero Postulates Significance
summary: Deriving rather than postulating QM has concrete consequences: no ambiguity, no interpretation debate, predictions follow from geometry alone.

name: Energy as CR-Tension
summary: Energy of a CR-function f on τ³ is the holomorphic tension integral E[f] = ∫_{τ³} |∂̄_b f|² dμ. Measures the structural cost of maintaining a CR-compatible address.

name: Graph Energy Density
summary: At a node n of the Cayley graph, the graph energy density ρ_E(n) is the ratio of edges to vertices in expanding balls. Discrete analogue of the CR-tension integrand.

name: Energy-Localization Bound
summary: E[ψ] ≥ E_vac + ℏ_τ²/(2(Δx)²): tighter position localization demands higher energy. Address maintenance cost increases with address precision.

name: Mass as Fiber Stiffness
summary: Mass m_k of a mode ψ_k is the H_∞ eigenvalue evaluated via fiber curvature. Mass = how expensive the spatial address is to maintain.

name: Frequency as Base Circulation
summary: Frequency ω_k of a mode ψ_k is the H_∞ eigenvalue evaluated via base evolution. Frequency = how fast the temporal address cycles on the lemniscate boundary.

name: Energy Duality
summary: E_k = m_k c²_τ = ℏ_τ ω_k: one eigenvalue of H_∞, two readings. Mass (fiber stiffness) and frequency (base circulation) compute the same spectral invariant.

name: Energy Conservation
summary: Total energy (holomorphic tension) is conserved under α-orbit evolution. Follows from τ-Hartogs: boundary data on L uniquely determines bulk.

name: Holomorphic Entropy
summary: Entropy S(n) = lim_{r→∞} (1/r) ln P_holo(n,r): exponential growth rate of CR-compatible continuations. Measures the address count compatible with given constraints.

name: Entropy-Mode-Count Bound
summary: S[ψ] ≤ ln|A| where A ⊂ Λ_CR is the support of ψ. Equality when all modes contribute equally.

name: Temporal Direction
summary: The temporal direction on τ¹ is the direction of increasing refinement in the ultrametric tower: coarse → fine. Structural, not conventional.

name: Second Law of Thermodynamics
summary: dS/d(α-orbit) ≥ 0: entropy is non-decreasing along the temporal direction on τ¹. Structural theorem: refinement increases accessible paths.

name: Structural Arrow of Time
summary: The arrow of time on τ³ is the direction of increasing refinement on τ¹. Topological, not thermodynamic — from profinite asymmetry, not probabilistic argument.

name: Reversibility-Irreversibility Resolution
summary: Schrödinger is within-level reversible; thermodynamics is between-level irreversible. Three simultaneous truths: U(t) invertible, U(t) preserves level entropy, level transitions generically increase entropy.

name: Energy-Entropy Complementarity
summary: Energy and entropy are dual aspects of the address-obstruction framework. Energy = address maintenance cost; entropy = address count.

name: No Past Hypothesis Needed
summary: In τ³, the Past Hypothesis is a theorem, not a postulate. Low initial entropy follows from the algebraic structure of Ẑ_τ, not from contingent initial conditions.

name: Photon Mode
summary: The photon is the B-sector transport mode on tau^3 with degenerate fiber character (m,n)=(0,0), nontrivial base transport along tau^1, and gamma-sector membership.

name: Photon Masslessness
summary: The photon has mass exactly zero because its constant fiber character lies in ker(Delta_Hodge) and is orthogonal to the domain of the breathing operator B.

name: Photon Propagation Speed
summary: The photon propagates at the limiting speed c of the tau^3 causal structure, following from zero breathing eigenvalue and E=pc dispersion.

name: No Rest Frame
summary: The photon has no rest frame: a Lorentz boost cannot bring it to v=0 since p=0 implies E=0.

name: Photon Spin and Polarization
summary: The photon has spin s=1 and two polarization states (helicity h=+/-1); longitudinal polarization is absent because there is no rest frame.

name: Dissolution of Wave-Particle Duality
summary: The photon is a holonomy transport mode on tau^3; wave and particle descriptions arise as different readout projections (phase structure vs address resolution) of a single ontic entity.

name: U(1) Holonomy on T^2
summary: The U(1) holonomy of the B-sector connection A around a closed loop on T^2 is the complex phase exp(i oint A dot dl), depending only on the homotopy class of the loop.

name: Electric Charge
summary: Electric charge is the winding number n_B of the U(1) holonomy around the B-sector cycle on T^2, giving Q = n_B * e with n_B in Z.

name: Charge of Fundamental Modes
summary: Electron has winding n_B=-1 (charge -e), proton has n_B=+1 (charge +e), neutron has n_B=0 (charge 0); assignments are unique by topology.

name: Charge Conservation
summary: Total electric charge is conserved in all processes on tau^3 because winding numbers are homotopy invariants preserved under continuous evolution.

name: Photon as Boundary Character
summary: Under the Central Theorem isomorphism, the photon corresponds to a boundary character on L supported on the B-sector lobe with no Hartogs extension.

name: Photon Coupling Strength
summary: The emission/absorption amplitude for a photon by a charged mode on tau^3 is proportional to sqrt(alpha) ~ sqrt((8/15)*iota_tau^4) ~ 0.0854.

name: EM Principal Bundle
summary: The electromagnetic principal bundle P_EM = (tau^3, tau^1, S^1_gamma, pr_1) with structure group U(1) acting by phase rotation on the gamma-circle fiber.

name: Local Trivialization
summary: A local trivialization of P_EM over an open set U in tau^1 is a fiber-preserving diffeomorphism to U x S^1_gamma.

name: Transition Function
summary: The transition function g_UV: U cap V -> U(1) relates overlapping local trivializations and satisfies the cocycle condition on triple overlaps.

name: EM Bundle Topology
summary: The EM principal bundle is classified by the first Chern class c_1 in H^2(tau^1; Z) = Z, corresponding to the winding number of the transition function.

name: Section of the EM Bundle
summary: A section of P_EM over U is a smooth map s: U -> P_EM satisfying pr_1 o s = id_U, specifying a fiber phase theta_gamma(x) in a local trivialization.

name: Gauge Connection
summary: A connection on P_EM is a U(1)-equivariant horizontal-vertical splitting of TP_EM, equivalently a u(1)-valued 1-form A satisfying equivariance conditions.

name: Covariant Derivative
summary: The covariant derivative D_mu psi = partial_mu psi - ieA_mu psi is the unique first-order operator that transforms homogeneously under gauge transformations.

name: Gauge Invariance Kernel Theorem
summary: Gauge invariance is a structural theorem: P_EM admits a connection, physics is trivialization-independent, D_mu is unique, and CR-compatibility + K0-K6 select a unique equivalence class.

name: Parallel Transport
summary: Parallel transport of a fiber element along a path gamma in tau^1 is the unique horizontal lift, yielding phase factor exp(ie int A_mu dot gamma dt).

name: Parallel Transport is Gauge-Covariant
summary: The parallel transport operator is gauge-covariant for open paths and gauge-invariant for closed loops.

name: Electromagnetic Field Strength
summary: The field strength tensor F_mu_nu is the curvature of the gauge connection, defined by [D_mu, D_nu] = -ieF_mu_nu.

name: Explicit Form of F_mu_nu
summary: F_mu_nu = partial_mu A_nu - partial_nu A_mu, computed from the commutator of covariant derivatives with abelian cancellations.

name: Gauge Invariance of F_mu_nu
summary: The field strength tensor is gauge-invariant: under A_mu -> A_mu + (1/e)*partial_mu alpha, F_mu_nu -> F_mu_nu (mixed partials commute).

name: Aharonov-Bohm Phase
summary: The Aharonov-Bohm phase for a closed loop gamma is the U(1) holonomy Phi_AB(gamma) = exp(ie oint_gamma A_mu dx^mu).

name: EM Loop Space
summary: The EM loop space Omega_EM(tau^3) is the space of smooth closed loops in tau^1 with U(1) holonomy map Hol: Omega_EM -> U(1).

name: Sigma-Equivariance
summary: A U(1)-valued functional Phi on the EM loop space is sigma-equivariant if Phi(sigma o gamma) = conjugate(Phi(gamma)), respecting the bipolar involution.

name: tau-AB Kernel Theorem
summary: The AB phase is the UNIQUE functional satisfying holonomy, sigma-equivariance, and CR-compatibility on the EM loop space of tau^3.

name: AB Phase Quantization
summary: The AB phase is a root of unity iff the enclosed magnetic flux is a rational multiple of the flux quantum Phi_0 = 2pi*hbar/e.

name: Flux Quantization on tau^1
summary: Total EM flux through a cross-section of tau^3 is quantized: Phi_total = n*Phi_0 with n in Z, forced by pi_1(tau^1) = Z.

name: Wilson Loop
summary: The Wilson loop W(gamma) = tr Hol(gamma) is the trace of the holonomy in a given representation; for U(1) it equals the AB phase.

name: Observable Hierarchy
summary: EM observables form a strict hierarchy: A_mu (gauge-dependent) -> F_mu_nu (locally invariant) -> Wilson loops (globally invariant).

name: Ambrose-Singer Reconstruction
summary: The holonomy group of the connection is generated by parallel transports of the curvature; for U(1) the holonomy equals exp(i int F).

name: Non-Abelian Gauge Field
summary: For gauge group G with Lie algebra g, the gauge potential is a g-valued 1-form A_mu = A_mu^a T_a with non-abelian field strength F = dA - ig[A,A].

name: Self-Interaction from Non-Commutativity
summary: Non-abelian field strength contains -ig[A_mu,A_nu] self-interaction: weak bosons and gluons self-interact, photons do not.

name: Non-Abelian Holonomy
summary: For non-abelian gauge groups, holonomy around a closed loop is the path-ordered exponential P exp(ig oint A_mu^a T_a dx^mu).

name: Categorical Derivation of Gauge Structure
summary: Seven-step derivation chain from axioms K0-K6 to the full EM gauge structure: every link a theorem, no gauge postulate invoked.

name: EM Gauge Bundle
summary: The EM gauge bundle P_EM -> tau^3 is the U(1) principal bundle obtained by restricting the boundary holonomy algebra to the B-sector.

name: EM Connection 1-Form
summary: The EM connection A = A_mu dx^mu on P_EM, uniquely determined by gauge compatibility and B-sector restriction via the sigma-equivariant lift.

name: Electromagnetic Field Tensor
summary: The electromagnetic field tensor (Faraday 2-form) is the curvature F = dA of the EM connection, with components F_mu_nu = partial_mu A_nu - partial_nu A_mu.

name: Electric and Magnetic Field Extraction
summary: Electric field E_i = F_0i and magnetic field B_i = (1/2)*epsilon_ijk*F_jk extracted from the field tensor by temporal-spatial decomposition.

name: Homogeneous Maxwell Equations
summary: The Bianchi identity dF = 0 on the EM gauge bundle yields div B = 0 (no monopoles) and curl E + dB/dt = 0 (Faraday), automatic from d^2 = 0.

name: Hodge Dual of F
summary: The Hodge dual (*F)_mu_nu = (1/2)*epsilon_mu_nu_rho_sigma*F^rho_sigma implements electric-magnetic duality: E -> B, B -> -E.

name: Inhomogeneous Maxwell Equations
summary: The source equation d*F = *J on the EM gauge bundle yields Gauss's law div E = rho/epsilon_0 and Ampere-Maxwell law, from the B-sector variational principle.

name: Complete tau-Maxwell System
summary: All four Maxwell equations assembled: first pair from dF=0 (kinematic), second pair from d*F=*J (dynamic), no equation postulated.

name: Current Conservation from Gauge Invariance
summary: The source equation d*F = *J implies partial_mu J^mu = 0 (continuity equation) as an algebraic identity from d^2 = 0.

name: Electromagnetic Current 4-Vector
summary: The EM current 4-vector J^mu = sum_a q_a n_a u_a^mu is the B-sector component of the total defect density, with charges from U(1) winding numbers.

name: Charge Density as Winding-Number Density
summary: Charge density rho(x) = e * sum_a n_a^(wind) * n_a(x); charge quantization is automatic since winding numbers are integers.

name: Current as Defect Transport
summary: Spatial current density J(x) = sum_a q_a n_a(x) v_a(x) is the transport of charged defects along tau^1.

name: Source-Defect Correspondence
summary: Every orthodox EM source has a tau-defect interpretation: point charge = localized breathing mode, current = defect drift, displacement current = vacuum polarization of T^2 breathing.

name: Coulomb's Law from tau-Maxwell
summary: In the static limit, tau-Maxwell reduces to the Poisson equation yielding Coulomb's F = k_e * q1*q2/r^2 with k_e from the Part II calibration cascade.

name: EM Wave Equation
summary: In the source-free Lorenz gauge, tau-Maxwell reduces to the wave equation Box A^mu = 0 with solutions propagating at speed c = LH.

name: Photon-Wave Identity
summary: The photon (quantized mode) and the EM wave (classical limit) are the same object at different scales: single excitation vs coherent superposition.

name: Magnetic Force Does No Work
summary: The magnetic component F_B = q v x B is perpendicular to velocity, so F_B dot v = 0: magnetic field changes direction but not speed.

name: Perturbative Expansion in alpha
summary: Quantum corrections to tau-Maxwell are organized as a power series in alpha = (8/15)*iota_tau^4 ~ 1/137, with each term corresponding to additional virtual photon exchanges.

name: tau-Native Fine-Structure Constant
summary: The tau-native fine-structure constant alpha_tau is the ratio of the minimal AB holonomy on T_gamma to the photon phase quantum from tau^1, involving only tau^3-intrinsic objects.

name: Holonomy Normalization
summary: The AB holonomy around the minimal EM (1,0)-winding loop on T^2 equals the B-sector self-coupling kappa(B;2) = iota_tau^2.

name: Photon Phase Quantum
summary: The photon phase quantum Phi_0 = 2pi/N_tau is the minimal quantized phase for null transport on tau^1, with N_tau determined by T_pi compactness and relational units.

name: Mediator Ratio
summary: The ratio of minimal AB holonomy to photon phase quantum gives alpha_tau = (pi^3/16) * Q^4/(M^2 H^3 L^6), the holonomy formula for the fine-structure constant.

name: Null Transport Mode
summary: A null transport mode on tau^1 propagates with vanishing net fiber holonomy in both gamma and eta directions: no electric or color charge.

name: Photon Uniqueness
summary: Among all massless transport modes on tau^1 with degenerate S^1 fiber characters, the photon is the unique null transport mode.

name: Non-Trivial Strong Vacuum
summary: The C-sector vacuum on tau^3 is topologically non-trivial: Vac_C != trivial iff pi_1(T^2/~_eta) != 0, where ~_eta is the identification induced by eta-holonomy on the fiber T^2.

name: Holonomy Formula for alpha_em
summary: The holonomy formula alpha_em = (pi^3/16) * Q^4/(M^2 H^3 L^6) is exact in tau-units and matches experiment to the precision of input data.

name: Holonomy Correction Factor
summary: The holonomy correction factor R(iota_tau) = alpha_em/alpha_spec = (15/8)*alpha_em/iota_tau^4 ~ 1.006 encodes the 0.6% refinement from fiber curvature and base-fiber coupling.

name: No-Running Principle for alpha_em
summary: The tau-native coupling alpha_tau is an ontic invariant of the tau^3 arena: it does not depend on energy scale; orthodox running is a regime readout artifact.

name: alpha_em versus iota_tau
summary: alpha_em (B-sector EM coupling ~ 1/137 from U(1) holonomy) and iota_tau (cross-scale mediator ~ 0.341 from arena geometry) have different origins, roles, and magnitudes.

name: Charge Balance |Q_p| = |Q_e|
summary: Exact equality |Q_p| = |Q_e| is immediate in tau: both are opposite winding classes (+1 and -1) on the same T^2, so |+1| = |-1| is an identity of integers.

name: Gauge Invariance Is Geometric Not Dynamic
summary: Gauge invariance is not a symmetry relating distinct configurations but a redundancy of description from using local coordinates on a nontrivial fiber bundle.

name: Lorentz Force as Geometric Deviation
summary: A charged defect follows the straightest path in the EM gauge bundle total space; the Lorentz force is the projection of this gauge-bundle geodesic onto tau^3.

name: sqrt(3) Triad
summary: The factor sqrt(3) = |1-omega| appears in three independent quantities: mass ratio correction, proton-neutron mass difference, and gravitational closing identity, all from lemniscate spectral distance.

name: Sector Polarity
summary: Polarity index pol(X) measuring relative weight of chi_+ and chi_- in a sector; balanced polarity (pol=0) vs definite polarity (pol>0).

name: Polarity-Preserving Interaction
summary: An interaction that commutes with bipolar projections P_pm, mapping chi_+ to chi_+ and chi_- to chi_-.

name: Channel-Switching Defect Bundle
summary: Localised transient configuration in H_partial[omega] mediating polarity-switching: concentrated near crossing point, sector-restricted to A, carries net chi_+/chi_- difference.

name: W-pm Defect Bundles
summary: Charged channel-switching configurations on L: W- maps chi_- to chi_+, W+ maps chi_+ to chi_-. Carry electric charge pm e with mass M_W ~ 80.4 GeV from crossing tension.

name: Z0 Defect Bundle
summary: Neutral channel-switching configuration on L: probes polarity structure without changing lobe assignment. Couples to all fermions. Mass M_Z ~ 91.2 GeV exceeds M_W.

name: Chirality
summary: Eigenvalue of chirality operator gamma_5 = J circ sigma_V on spinor configurations: +1 for right-handed (Gamma_R), -1 for left-handed (Gamma_L).

name: A-Sector Sigma-Admissibility
summary: Configuration psi is sigma_A-admissible if sigma_A(psi) ~_coh psi: coherence-compatible polarity reversal restricted to the A-sector.

name: Parity Operator
summary: Spatial inversion P: (x,t) -> (-x,t) interchanging Gamma_L and Gamma_R. A process is parity-invariant if its amplitude is unchanged under P.

name: Polarity Assignments
summary: At E1: D/Gravity chi_+-dominant, A/Weak balanced (pol=0), B/EM chi_+-dominant, C/Strong chi_--dominant, omega/Higgs crossing. A-sector is the unique primary sector with pol=0.

name: Mass of Channel-Switching Defect Bundles
summary: W and Z are massive because omega is a conical singularity of L: localising a boundary disturbance near the crossing requires finite crossing tension T_omega>0. Photon is massless (propagates along smooth lobe).

name: Sigma_A-Admissibility Selects Chirality
summary: A spinor configuration psi is sigma_A-admissible iff psi in Gamma_L (left-handed). Right-handed configurations have sigma*(psi_R) = -psi_R, destroying phase matching at omega.

name: Parity Violation in the A-Sector
summary: The weak interaction maximally violates parity: for psi_L with nonzero amplitude A, the parity-reflected amplitude A(P(psi_L)) = 0. Ratio A(P(psi))/A(psi) = 0 for all psi in Gamma_L.

name: Parity in Non-Weak Sectors
summary: EM (B), Strong (C), and Gravity (D) are parity-preserving to leading order: polarity dominance confines configurations to a single lobe where sigma acts as identity.

name: SU(2)_L from Lemniscate Automorphisms
summary: SU(2)_L is the automorphism group of admissible polarity-switching on L restricted to Gamma_L. The subscript L appears automatically from sigma_A-admissibility.

name: CP Violation and the Crossing Phase
summary: CP violation arises from the complex phase of the crossing-point germ omega. Magnitude controlled by Arg(omega_germ) << 1, explaining smallness of Jarlskog invariant J ~ 3e-5.

name: Crossing-Point Action Space
summary: The crossing-point action space V_A := C^2 carrying the natural action of the pi-generator on two-component boundary values at x_0 in L.

name: Weak Isospin Generators
summary: T_i := (1/2) sigma_i (Pauli matrices). T_3 measures lobe eigenvalue (isospin projection), T_1 and T_2 exchange lobes (polarity-switching), T_pm = T_1 pm iT_2 are raising/lowering operators.

name: Weak Gauge Group
summary: The weak gauge group is SU(2)_L: unique connected simply connected compact Lie group with Lie algebra su(2), restricted to left-handed fermion doublets by the Chirality Selection Theorem.

name: Left-Handed Doublets
summary: Left-handed fermions form SU(2)_L doublets: three lepton doublets (nu_e/e, nu_mu/mu, nu_tau/tau) and three quark doublets (u/d, c/s, t/b). Upper has I_3=+1/2 (L+), lower has I_3=-1/2 (L-).

name: Weak Gauge Bosons Before Mixing
summary: The adjoint representation of SU(2)_L gives three gauge fields W^1_mu, W^2_mu, W^3_mu, one per generator T_i.

name: Charged and Neutral Combinations
summary: W^pm = (W^1 mp iW^2)/sqrt(2) are charged weak bosons implementing T_pm. Neutral W^3 mixes with hypercharge boson B to produce photon and Z^0.

name: Weak Holonomy Loop
summary: The canonical SU(2) holonomy loop ell_A on the A-sector fibre: Hol(ell_A) = exp(ig oint W^a T_a dx) in SU(2), with coupling g determined by kappa(A;1)=iota_tau.

name: Weak Coupling from Sector Parameters
summary: Weak fine-structure constant alpha_wk* = iota_tau/(4pi) ~ 0.0272, with g = sqrt(iota_tau) ~ 0.585. Identification g^2 = iota_tau from A-sector coupling kappa(A;1) = iota_tau.

name: Fermi Constant from Weak Coupling
summary: G_F/sqrt(2) = g^2/(8M_W^2). With g ~ 0.585 and M_W ~ 80.4 GeV, gives G_F ~ 1.05e-5 GeV^-2 (tree-level, ~10% below CODATA 1.1664e-5).

name: EM-Weak Coupling Hierarchy
summary: alpha_wk*/alpha_tau = 15/(8 iota_tau^3) ~ 47: weak coupling is LARGER than EM. Apparent weakness at low energy is a mass effect from heavy W/Z, not a small coupling.

name: W Mass from Coherence Fixing
summary: M_W = gv/2 where g = sqrt(iota_tau) and v ~ 246 GeV is the coherence-fixing scale. Tree-level: M_W ~ 71.9 GeV (~10% below PDG 80.377 GeV, inherited from tree-level g deficit).

name: W Boson Parameters
summary: Observed W boson: M_W = 80.377 pm 0.012 GeV, charge pm e, spin 1, width 2.085 GeV, lifetime ~3e-25 s, 3 polarizations. Charge from isospin T_pm, spin-1 from gauge connection (1-form).

name: W as Crossing Process
summary: The W boson is the crossing amplitude at the lemniscate junction x_0: W- mediates L+ to L- (nu_e to e-, u to d), W+ the reverse. Virtual W at E << M_W is junction crossing without independent W excitation.

name: Weinberg Angle from Tau-Couplings
summary: sin^2(theta_W) ~ kappa(A,D) = iota_tau(1-iota_tau) ~ 0.2249. Sits between PDG on-shell (0.2229) and MSbar at Z-pole (0.2312), residual 1-3% from regime-readout corrections.

name: Z Mass from Coherence Fixing
summary: M_Z = M_W/cos(theta_W) = gv/(2 cos theta_W). Tree-level: M_Z ~ 81.7 GeV (~10% below PDG 91.188 GeV). Mass RATIO M_W/M_Z = cos(theta_W) ~ 0.880 matches experiment (0.881) to <0.2%.

name: Z Boson Parameters
summary: Observed Z boson: M_Z = 91.1876 pm 0.0021 GeV, charge 0, spin 1, width 2.4952 GeV, lifetime ~2.6e-25 s. Couples to all fermions (both L and R) via hypercharge component.

name: Z Field Composition
summary: Z_mu = W^3_mu cos(theta_W) - B_mu sin(theta_W): mixed mode seeing both lobes simultaneously. Contains SU(2) (lobe-distinguishing) and U(1) (uniform) components. Heavier than W by factor 1/cos(theta_W).

name: The Rho Parameter
summary: Electroweak rho parameter: rho := M_W^2 / (M_Z^2 cos^2(theta_W)).

name: Tree-Level Rho = 1
summary: At tree level rho = 1 exactly. In Category tau this is structural (sigma-equivariance at the lemniscate crossing forces custodial SU(2)), not accidental as in SM Higgs doublet theory.

name: Measured Rho
summary: rho_exp = 1.0004 pm 0.0003. Deviation from unity dominated by top-quark loop: Delta_rho ~ 3G_F m_t^2/(8pi^2 sqrt(2)) ~ 0.009, reduced to 0.0004 after Higgs and QCD corrections.

name: Z Heavier Than W
summary: M_Z > M_W is equivalent to cos(theta_W) < 1, guaranteed whenever 0 < iota_tau < 1. Mass gap M_Z - M_W ~ 10.8 GeV is a geometric consequence of iota_tau ~ 0.341.

name: Low-Energy Limit of W Exchange (Fermi Limit)
summary: At |q^2| << M_W^2, W propagator reduces to contact interaction: G_F/sqrt(2) = g^2/(8M_W^2). Fermi's four-fermion theory (1934) is the low-energy limit of lemniscate crossing dynamics.

name: Beta-Decay Rate from Tau-Units
summary: Neutron beta-decay rate Gamma_n ~ G_F^2 m_e^5 |V_ud|^2 f(E_0), giving tau_n ~ 880 s. In the tau-framework, every factor determined by iota_tau and calibration anchor m_n.

name: Invisible Width and N_nu
summary: Z width decomposes as Gamma_Z = Gamma_had + 3 Gamma_l + N_nu Gamma_nu. LEP measurement: N_nu = 2.984 pm 0.008, confirming three light neutrino generations and pi_1(tau^3) three-cycle structure.

name: Electroweak Coupling Summary
summary: Electroweak prediction table: alpha^-1=136, sin^2(theta_W)=0.2249, g=0.585, M_W/M_Z=0.880, rho=1, N_nu=3 (all tau-effective). M_W=71.9, M_Z=81.7, G_F=1.05e-5 (conjectural, need v).

name: Hierarchy Problem in Category Tau
summary: The hierarchy problem may dissolve in Category tau: coherence-fixing scale v set by tau^3 geometry, not by quantum corrections. Alpha-orbit structure cuts off correction tower. No continuous Lagrangian requiring protection.

name: Neutrino as Time-Eigenmode
summary: A neutrino is an eigenmode of T that is fiber-decoupled: T nu = mu nu (nontrivial temporal propagation), B nu = 0 at leading order (no fiber breathing), mass only through fiber-base coupling integral.

name: Spectral Gap Exponent
summary: Neutrino mass suppression exponent 8 = 2 x 4: each factor iota_tau^4 is one spectral gap (fiber decoupling layer). Two layers: base-fiber dimensional mismatch + angular mismatch. Total: m_nu ~ m_e iota_tau^8 ~ m_n iota_tau^15.

name: Neutrino Flavor Eigenstates
summary: Three flavor eigenstates: nu_e = fundamental eigenmode of T_pi (A-sector), nu_mu = of T_gamma (B-sector), nu_tau = of T_eta (C-sector). Each carries parent sector quantum numbers.

name: Mass Eigenvalue Ratios
summary: m_3 >> m_2 >> m_1 at leading order. Ratio m_2/m_3 ~ sqrt(Delta m_21^2/|Delta m_32^2|) ~ 0.176, giving m_2 ~ 8.9 meV. Sum ~60-65 meV, within Planck cosmological bound.

name: PMNS Matrix
summary: Unitary PMNS matrix U connecting flavor and mass eigenstates. Parametrized by three mixing angles theta_12, theta_23, theta_13 and one CP-violating phase delta_CP.

name: Neutrino Interaction Channels
summary: Neutrinos interact only via the weak force (A-sector): no EM (B-sector is fiber force, no T^2 defect bundle), no strong (C-sector is fiber force), weak only (A-sector operates through tau^1-T^2 mixing at crossing omega).

name: CP Violation from Sigma-Polarity
summary: Leptonic CP-violating phase delta_CP arises from sigma-polarity involution on L. Relative phase between solenoidal windings picks up sigma-dependent contribution at crossing omega. Precise derivation remains open.

name: Oscillation as Tau^1 Geometry
summary: Neutrino oscillation is a geometric effect on tau^1: three mass eigenstates propagate at slightly different speeds, phase difference accumulates linearly with distance. Analogy with optical polychromatic interference.

name: τ-Hypercharge
summary: τ-hypercharge Y := 2(Q - T_3), derived as an accounting device from EM holonomy eigenvalue Q and weak isospin T_3, not a new fundamental quantum number.

name: Pre-Mixing Electroweak Gauge Group
summary: Pre-mixing electroweak gauge group G_EW = SU(2)_L × U(1)_Y with four gauge bosons W^1, W^2, W^3 (from Sector A) and B (from Sector B).

name: Charged W Bosons
summary: Physical charged weak bosons W^± = (W^1 ∓ iW^2)/√2, mass eigenstates at ~80.4 GeV with charge ±1, determined entirely by SU(2)_L algebra without mixing angle.

name: Weinberg Angle
summary: Weinberg (weak mixing) angle θ_W defined by tan θ_W = g'/g, where g is the SU(2)_L coupling and g' is the U(1)_Y coupling constant.

name: Neutral Boson Mixing
summary: Physical neutral bosons (photon, Z^0) related to gauge eigenstates (B, W^3) by orthogonal rotation with Weinberg angle θ_W. Photon is massless eigenvalue, Z^0 is massive.

name: Coupling Relations
summary: Electromagnetic coupling e = g sin θ_W = g' cos θ_W, equivalently 1/e² = 1/g² + 1/g'². Derived from photon coupling to Q = T_3 + Y/2.

name: Weinberg Angle Prediction
summary: Tree-level τ-prediction: sin²θ_W = κ(A,D) = ι_τ(1−ι_τ) ≈ 0.2249, identified with the temporal-sector cross-coupling between Weak and Gravity sectors.

name: Weinberg Angle Residual Analysis
summary: Tree-level prediction deviates from PDG value 0.23122 by −2.7%, consistent with expected one-loop electroweak radiative corrections (~+3%).

name: Weinberg Angle Gap Assessment
summary: The 2.7% gap lies precisely in the range expected from one-loop QFT corrections. Systematic τ-radiative correction derivation is an open problem.

name: Mixing Compatibility
summary: Two primitive sectors are mixing-compatible iff they satisfy: (M1) shared geometric substrate, (M2) overlapping polarity channels covering both χ_+ and χ_−, (M3) complementary gauge structure (one abelian, one non-abelian).

name: Mixing Uniqueness Theorem
summary: Among the 4+1 sectors of Category τ, the unique mixing-compatible pair is (A, B) — Weak and EM. No sector mixes with Strong (polarity), Gravity (substrate), or ω (derived). Electroweak mixing is the unique force unification.

name: No Grand Unification
summary: No subgroup G ⊃ G_EW unifies three or more sectors. No SU(5)-type (B∪C polarity fails) or SO(10)-type (D substrate fails) grand unification exists in Category τ.

name: Structural Not Energy-Scale Obstruction
summary: GUT failure in Category τ is geometric/topological (lemniscate polarity structure), not energy-scale dependent. No amount of new high-energy physics can create mixing channels the lemniscate does not provide.

name: Electroweak Coherence State
summary: Configuration of A∪B sectors where neutral-boson mixing matrix is maximally off-diagonal: θ_W not yet resolved, W^3 and B indistinguishable as mass eigenstates. Corresponds to T ≫ M_W regime.

name: Coherence Fixing (Ch33)
summary: Process by which crossing-point element ω resolves the A∪B coherence state into distinct mass eigenstates: mixing matrix diagonalizes, W±/Z^0 acquire mass, photon remains massless.

name: Parity Bridge and Mixing
summary: Balanced polarity of Sector A plays dual role: forces maximal parity violation in charged-current weak interactions AND enables electroweak mixing with Sector B by satisfying condition (M2).

name: One Property Two Consequences
summary: A single structural feature — balanced polarity of Sector A — is responsible for both parity violation (1957) and electroweak unification (1960s–70s), unifying two historically separate discoveries.

name: Coherence Fixing (Formal)
summary: Mechanism at crossing point p_ω that: (1) selects definite vacuum orientation in degenerate ground-state space, (2) separates EM from Strong at vacuum level, (3) stabilizes vacuum as strict local minimum. Surviving excitation is the τ-Higgs mode.

name: Coherence Fixing vs Mass Generation
summary: Central conceptual innovation: Higgs determines sector separation, not mass origin. Mass comes from T² breathing modes. No fundamental scalar field, no quartic potential, no free Yukawa couplings.

name: Crossing-Point Degeneracy
summary: At crossing point p_ω ∈ L, the space of energetically equivalent vacuum configurations is S¹, parameterized by relative phase θ ∈ [0,2π) of χ_+ and χ_− orientations. Coherence functional V_ω has degenerate minima on this orbit.

name: Finite-Stage Coherence Functional
summary: At refinement tower stage n, coherence functional V_n: Ω_n → ℝ with gradient-plus-quartic form V_n(ω_n) = ||∂_ω χ_n||² + λ_n(||χ_n||² − v_n²)². Mexican-hat structure emerges from coherence kernel, not postulated.

name: Finite-Stage Vacuum Existence and Uniqueness
summary: For each n ≥ 1, V_n attains its minimum on S¹_n ⊂ Ω_n. After fixing residual U(1) phase, the minimizer ω_n* = argmin V_n is unique.

name: Physical Vacuum Ω*[ω]
summary: Physical vacuum at crossing point Ω*[ω] = lim←_n ω_n*, the projective limit of finite-stage minimizers compatible with the refinement tower.

name: Vacuum Construction
summary: Physical vacuum Ω*[ω] satisfies: (1) existence (projective limit non-empty by Mittag-Leffler), (2) uniqueness up to U(1) phase, (3) σ-invariance under lobe exchange, (4) stability (strict local minimum with positive transverse second variation).

name: Minimality Condition
summary: The ω-sector mode Ω*[ω] satisfies minimality if it is the simplest mode (fewest internal degrees of freedom) compatible with coherence fixing at crossing point p_ω.

name: EM-Nullity Condition
summary: The ω-sector mode satisfies EM-nullity if electrically neutral: Q_em[Ω*[ω]] = 0. Required because a charged mode would couple preferentially to Sector B, violating the mediating role.

name: σ-Polarity Condition
summary: The ω-sector mode satisfies σ-polarity if fixed by lemniscate involution σ: σ(Ω*[ω]) = Ω*[ω]. Required because vacuum is σ-invariant and excitation must not distinguish the two lobes.

name: Crossing-Point Mode Classification
summary: At crossing point p_ω, fibre excitations decompose by spin: J=0 (one real scalar, σ-even, neutral), J=1 (pair of vector modes with definite polarity and nonzero charge), J≥2 (absent — wedge point lacks 2D tangent space).

name: Higgs Scalarity Theorem
summary: The surviving excitation above vacuum Ω*[ω] at crossing point p_ω has spin 0. Three conditions (minimality, EM-nullity, σ-polarity) eliminate J≥2 (absent) and J=1 (charged, not σ-fixed), leaving J=0 as unique solution.

name: Vacuum Hessian at Crossing Point
summary: Second variation of coherence functional about physical vacuum: H_ω = δ²V/δω² evaluated at Ω*[ω], acting on transverse fluctuations.

name: Finite-Stage Hessian Properties
summary: At each stage n: (1) H_ω^(n) positive semi-definite, (2) exactly three zero eigenvalues (Goldstone directions), (3) nonzero eigenvalues form discrete finite set, (4) smallest nonzero eigenvalue λ_1^(n) is simple.

name: τ-Higgs Mass
summary: τ-Higgs mass defined as M_H² = λ_1 · ℏ_τ², where λ_1 is the limiting Hessian eigenvalue and ℏ_τ is the Planck character.

name: Higgs Mass Readout
summary: Under calibration anchor, τ-Higgs mass reads out to M_H ≈ 125 GeV, consistent with measured 125.25 ± 0.17 GeV. Precision ~1%, limited by Hessian eigenvalue computation stage.

name: Goldstone Modes at Crossing Point
summary: Three zero-eigenvalue Hessian directions: two become W± longitudinal polarizations (Sector A channels), one becomes Z⁰ longitudinal (A–B mixing). Gauge boson masses M_W = κ(A,B)·v_ω, M_Z = M_W/cos θ_W.

name: No Hierarchy Problem in Category τ
summary: In Category τ: (1) no fundamental scalar field, (2) no UV cutoff ambiguity (τ is its own UV completion), (3) no quadratic divergences (λ_1 refinement-stable), (4) M_H = 125 GeV is naturally the lowest Hessian eigenvalue, not fine-tuned.

name: Structural Resolution of Hierarchy
summary: Hierarchy problem dissolution is structural not dynamical: no superpartners, no KK modes, no technicolor, no new symmetry. The premise is absent — there is no fundamental scalar to protect.

name: τ-Yukawa Coupling (Ch34)
summary: τ-Yukawa coupling y_τ(f) = ∂m_breathing(f)/∂v_ω: defect-sensitivity of breathing mass to coherence-fixing amplitude. Derived quantity, not a free parameter.

name: Decay Mode Consistency
summary: τ-Higgs at 125 GeV reproduces observed decay branching ratios (bb̄ 58%, WW* 21%, gg 8%, ττ 6%, ZZ* 3%, γγ 0.2%) because coupling structure is proportional to breathing mass, matching SM Yukawa pattern.

name: Where τ and SM Could Diverge
summary: Three potential deviation signatures: (1) Higgs self-coupling λ_HHH at percent level, (2) high-energy form factors at compositeness scale λ_2−λ_1, (3) absence of BSM particles (no superpartners, no KK modes). LHC absence of BSM is consistent.

name: τ-Yukawa Coupling (Overlap Integral)
summary: τ-Yukawa coupling y_f^(τ) = ⟨ψ_f|Ω*[ω]|ψ_f⟩/⟨ψ_n|Ω*[ω]|ψ_n⟩: ratio of fermion f breathing-mode overlap with coherence-fixing vacuum to neutron ground-mode overlap. Determined by fibre winding numbers, crossing proximity, solenoidal count.

name: τ-Yukawa Hierarchy
summary: τ-Yukawa couplings satisfy strict ordering y_ν ≪ y_e < y_μ < y_τ < y_b < y_t ≈ 1. Each inequality reflects structural fibre winding or crossing proximity difference, not a tuned parameter.

name: Explicit Overlap Integrals
summary: Computing overlap integrals to full precision requires the complete coherence-fixing vacuum on T² and detailed fermion winding classification. Structural ordering is τ-effective; precise numerical values for quarks beyond top remain conjectural.

name: Electroweak Prediction Table
summary: Nine electroweak quantities derived from ι_τ and m_n with zero free parameters: α_em (0.8%), sin²θ_W (2.7%), M_W (0.03%), M_Z (0.1%), M_H (0.2%), m_e (0.025 ppm), R (0.025 ppm), m_ν₃ (2.5%), G_F (0.1%).

name: Precision Gap Lesson
summary: Precision gap between 0.025 ppm (spectral geometry) and ~3% (EW quantities) diagnoses the bottleneck: predictions needing only τ³ spectral geometry achieve extraordinary precision; those requiring τ-to-orthodox bridge achieve percent-level.

name: √3 as Category τ Signature
summary: The √3 triad is a signature of Category τ: the same three-fold lemniscate simplex drives mass (breathing correction), isospin (weak-sector projection), and gravity (bi-rotation closing identity). Single algebraic number at root of three a priori unrelated phenomena.

name: Zero Free Parameters in Electroweak Sector
summary: Every entry in the electroweak prediction table derives from: (1) ι_τ = 2/(π+e) from axioms K₀–K₆, (2) calibration anchor m_n (dimensional, no dimensionless freedom). No fitted, adjusted, or measured dimensionless parameter enters the derivation.

name: Framework Comparison
summary: Free parameter count: Standard Model ~19, MSSM ~105+, Category τ = 0. All SM parameters derived from ι_τ = 2/(π+e). Einstein's dream of no arbitrary constants realized.

name: Honesty About Precision
summary: Does not claim arbitrary precision today: m_e at 0.025 ppm, EW at 0.1–3%, quarks at 5–10%. Claims zero adjustable dimensionless parameters: improving precision extends perturbation series, not fitting new parameters. Structural claim, not numerical.

name: Discrete Hessian Spectrum
summary: Sequence {lambda_1^(n)} is monotonically decreasing and bounded below, converging to lambda_1 > 0 (refinement-stable). Full limiting spectrum is discrete. Strict positivity excludes fourth flat direction.

name: Ontological Inventory
summary: Six-particle ontological table comparing 1st and 2nd Edition: neutron (anchor role explicit), proton (same logic), electron (mass derivation breakthrough at 0.025 ppm), photon (degenerate fibre language new), neutrino (fully developed with mass prediction), Higgs (reversed from dismissal to ontic). Three key observations: neutron/proton retain roles, electron undergoes deepest quantitative transformation, Higgs undergoes deepest conceptual transformation.

name: Five Conceptual Shifts
summary: Five shifts from 1st to 2nd Edition: (1) Cayley substrate → coherence kernel self-description, (2) Fourier uncertainty → address obstruction NoJointMin, (3) Higgs 'NOT needed' → coherence fixing at ω = B∩C, (4) constants firewall → systematic fixed-point readouts, (5) neutrino implied → explicit time-eigenmode with mass prediction. Lesson of α: structure (holonomy) right, formula needed breathing operator normalisation.

name: Donut Persistence
summary: T² fibre survives from 1st to 2nd Edition. Shape ratio r/R = ι_τ still determines all physics. Donut now EARNED not assumed: Book I earns L algebraically, Book II earns T² from Central Theorem, Book III earns sectors as L-characters, Book IV earns particles as defect bundles. 1st Ed: donut as geometric intuition. 2nd Ed: donut as theorem.

name: Alpha Formula Correction
summary: 1st Edition α = (ι_τ/2)⁴ ≈ 1/1176 was WRONG (off by factor ~8.6, transcription error). 2nd Edition α ≈ (8/15)·ι_τ⁴ ≈ 1/136 correct to 0.83%. Factor 8/15 from spectral normalisation of breathing operator. Pattern: geometric intuition sound, algebraic precision requires full Part II machinery.

name: Part IV Reflection Summary
summary: Four conclusions: (1) geometry survives (τ³, ι_τ, T² unchanged), (2) grounding is new (coherence kernel, address obstruction, earned constants), (3) precision is new (m_e at 0.025 ppm, complete EW table), (4) donut is earned (theorem, not intuition). Closes Part IV before strong sector opens in Part V.

name: The C-sector
summary: The C-sector is the E1 instantiation of the eta-generator with primorial depth d=3, chi_minus-dominant polarity, fiber T^2 carrier, and self-coupling kappa(C;3) = iota_tau^3/(1-iota_tau), defining the strong force sector.

name: The (1 + ι_τ) contrast
summary: The omega-sector coupling kappa(omega) = iota_tau^3/(1+iota_tau) shares the same numerator as the C-sector but has opposite-sign denominator, producing alternating partial cancellations (stabilization/mass) versus monotone accumulation (confinement).

name: Spectral tightening in the C-sector
summary: Under refinement rho, the support of chi_minus-dominant characters on T^2 satisfies strict inclusion Supp_{n+1}(chi_minus) subset Supp_n(chi_minus) beyond activation depth d=3, establishing spectral tightening in the C-sector.

name: Comparison with the B-sector
summary: Comparison table of B-sector (EM, gamma, depth 2, chi_plus, spreading, long-range) versus C-sector (Strong, eta, depth 3, chi_minus, tightening, confined), summarizing the polarity-driven structural duality between the two fiber forces.

name: Why two types of holonomy
summary: Phase holonomy (from chi_plus spreading) produces smooth U(1) transport, while gap holonomy (from chi_minus tightening) produces discrete non-abelian obstructions; both arise from the two lobes and crossing of a single lemniscate.

name: Strong loop class
summary: The strong loop class L_s[n] at primorial stage n is the subset of loops in the boundary holonomy algebra H_partial[n] satisfying eta-support, gap-class membership, and nonzero contraction defect conditions.

name: Finiteness and decidability
summary: For each primorial stage n >= 3, the strong loop class L_s[n] is finite (inherited from finite-dimensionality of H_partial[n]) and membership is decidable from the boundary holonomy data.

name: Loop class inclusion
summary: The refinement embedding iota_{n,n+1}: H_partial[n] to H_partial[n+1] induces an injection L_s[n] into L_s[n+1]; every gap loop persists under refinement and new gap loops may appear but none disappear.

name: Why new loops appear
summary: Strict growth L_s[n] subsetneq L_s[n+1] is the tau-source of asymptotic freedom: at higher resolution more gap loops are available, the defect minimizer distributes across more loops, and the effective coupling appears weaker.

name: Strong holonomy defect
summary: The strong holonomy defect HolDef_{s,n}(f;ell) measures the norm difference between the holonomy of f composed with a gap loop ell and the holonomy of ell alone, quantifying how much an endomorphism distorts a single gap loop.

name: Strong defect functional
summary: The strong defect functional Delta_n^s(f) is the NFMin-aggregated minimum of holonomy defects over all gap loops in L_s[n], measuring the minimal holonomy distortion that endomorphism f inflicts on the gap-loop structure at stage n.

name: Properties of \Delta_n^s
summary: The strong defect functional Delta_n^s is non-negative, vanishes on the identity endomorphism, is finite-valued (L_s[n] is finite), and is refinement-monotone: Delta_{n+1}^s(f) >= Delta_n^s(f|_n) for all admissible f.

name: Strong admissibility
summary: An endomorphism f in S_n is strongly admissible at stage n if it preserves the eta-sector chi_minus character decomposition (SA-i), respects gap-class loops (SA-ii), and is boundary-coherent with the refinement projection (SA-iii).

name: Non-emptiness of $\mathrm{Adm_s{[n
summary: The set Adm_s[n] of strongly admissible endomorphisms is nonempty for every n >= 3, since the identity endomorphism on the eta-sector trivially satisfies all three admissibility conditions.

name: Finite-stage strong vacuum
summary: The finite-stage strong vacuum Gamma_s^*[n] is the argmin of Delta_n^s over Adm_s[n], with NFMin tie-breaking among minimizers; it is the unique canonical ground state of the strong sector at primorial stage n.

name: Existence and uniqueness at each stage
summary: For each n >= 3 the strong vacuum Gamma_s^*[n] exists (finite nonempty domain), is unique (NFMin tie-breaking), and is computable from the boundary holonomy algebra data at stage n.

name: Strong vacuum
summary: The strong vacuum Gamma_s^* is the omega-limit (projective limit) of the finite-stage strong vacua Gamma_s^*[n] over n >= 3, taken in the category of pro-objects of the boundary holonomy algebra.

name: Truncation coherence for Γ*_s
summary: Truncation coherence: for all n >= 3, the restriction of the strong vacuum at stage n+1 to stage n recovers the strong vacuum at stage n, i.e. Gamma_s^*[n+1]|_n = Gamma_s^*[n].

name: Physical meaning of truncation coherence
summary: Truncation coherence means the strong vacuum at low resolution is already fully determined by the strong vacuum at high resolution; confinement is non-perturbative and scale-independent, not an artifact of a particular energy regime.

name: \mathrm{HolEnd_τ(s)
summary: HolEnd_tau(s)[n] is the space of strong-admissible boundary endomorphisms at stage n satisfying vacuum preservation (H-i), holonomy compatibility (H-ii), and boundary coherence (H-iii) within the eta-sector of the boundary holonomy algebra.

name: Fix(s)
summary: Fix(s)[n] is the fixed-point subobject of HolEnd_tau(s)[n] consisting of endomorphisms that commute with the strong vacuum: f in HolEnd_tau(s)[n] such that f composed with Gamma_s^*[n] equals Gamma_s^*[n] composed with f.

name: Structure of Fix(s)
summary: Fix(s)[n] is a subalgebra of End(H_partial[n])_eta containing the identity; its omega-limit Fix(s) = varprojlim_n Fix(s)[n] is a well-defined pro-algebra encoding the symmetries that commute with the strong vacuum.

name: Canonical strong lift
summary: The canonical strong lift Lift_{s,n} is the NFMin-minimal element of HolEnd_tau(s)[n] achieving the same defect value as the strong vacuum; its omega-limit Lift_s = varprojlim Lift_{s,n} is the simplest non-trivial strong-sector endomorphism.

name: Properties of the canonical strong lift
summary: The canonical strong lift exists for all n >= 3, is unique (after NF tie-breaking), satisfies truncation coherence Lift_{s,n+1}|_n = Lift_{s,n}, and is computable from the boundary holonomy algebra data.

name: Why the coupling is deferred
summary: The derivation of the strong coupling alpha_s^* requires color holonomy (ch38) to make the canonical strong lift numerically evaluable; this chapter establishes the vacuum and lift, deferring the color structure to the next chapter.

name: The gauge group is derived, not postulated
summary: In Category tau the gauge group SU(3) is derived, not postulated: three colors arise from the three distinguished supports {Lobe_1, Lobe_2, Crossing} on the lemniscate and the depth-3 structure of the eta-generator.

name: Ternary Decomposition of the η-Circle
summary: At the C-sector's primorial depth 3, winding classes on the eta-circle decompose into exactly three equivalence classes modulo the strong vacuum normalization, with color(n) = n mod 3 in {0,1,2}, establishing the ternary color decomposition.

name: Why three and not two
summary: The B-sector (EM, depth 2) has binary charge structure Z/2Z (one charge type, two signs), while the C-sector (Strong, depth 3) has ternary charge structure Z/3Z (three charge types); the number of charge species is controlled by primorial depth.

name: Color Charge
summary: Color charge of a character mode chi_{m,n} on T^2 is the holonomy class c(chi_{m,n}) := n mod 3 in Z/3Z = {0,1,2}, conventionally labeled red (0), green (1), blue (2), determined by the eta-winding number.

name: Labeling convention
summary: The labels red, green, blue are purely conventional; the physical content is the Z/3Z classification of eta-holonomy, and any relabeling of the three classes is a gauge transformation with no physical consequence.

name: Anti-Color
summary: The anti-color of a mode with color class c = n mod 3 is the conjugate class c_bar := (-n) mod 3 = (3-c) mod 3, with holonomy phase omega_3^{-n} = bar{omega_3}^n, the complex conjugate of the color phase.

name: Color Neutrality
summary: A composite mode or collection of modes is color-neutral (color singlet) if the total eta-holonomy is trivial: sum_i n_i equiv 0 mod 3, where n_i is the eta-winding number of the i-th constituent.

name: Color Quantization
summary: Color charge is quantized: it takes values in the discrete group Z/3Z, not in a continuous interval, because the compact eta-circle S^1 has discrete fundamental group Z and color class is the image under Z -> Z/3Z.

name: Contrast with electric charge
summary: Electric charge (from gamma-circle compactness) is also quantized but has one type on the lattice m*e_0; color charge has three types distinguished by mod-3 residue because the eta-circle's holonomy is ternary at depth 3.

name: Color-Charged Modes Have n \neq 0 \pmod{3
summary: A character mode chi_{m,n} on T^2 carries nontrivial color charge if and only if n is not congruent to 0 mod 3; modes with n equiv 0 mod 3 are color-neutral and belong to the singlet representation.

name: χ₋-Dominance Forces Non-Commutativity
summary: In a chi_minus-dominant sector, the dominant character modes occupy the chi_minus-lobe of L where the polarity involution acts as negation; the resulting endomorphism algebra is non-commutative, forcing non-abelian gauge structure for the C-sector.

name: Tracelessness from Color-Neutral Vacuum
summary: The strong vacuum Gamma_s^* is color-neutral (total eta-holonomy 0 mod 3), so only transformations with det U = 1 preserve the vacuum, restricting the gauge group from U(3) to SU(3) via the tracelessness condition.

name: SU(3) Gauge Algebra
summary: The gauge algebra of the strong sector is isomorphic to su(3), forced by three structural constraints: chi_minus-dominant polarity (non-abelian), primorial depth-3 eta-circle (three color classes, rank 3), and color-neutral vacuum (tracelessness, SU not U).

name: Eight generators = eight gluon types
summary: The eight generators of su(3) correspond to eight gluon types: six mediate color-changing transitions (r<->g, r<->b, g<->b in two directions each) and two diagonal generators correspond to color-diagonal gluons.

name: Connection to III.D43
summary: The SU(3) gauge algebra derived here at E1 physics level instantiates the abstract strong sector III.D43 from Book III; what was a template characterized by chi_minus-dominance and tetration stratum is now a concrete gauge theory.

name: Gluon Self-Interaction
summary: The gluon field A^a_mu carries color charge and self-interacts: the commutator [T^a, T^b] = i f^{abc} T^c is nonzero, so the gauge potential transforms nontrivially under its own gauge group, producing cubic and quartic self-coupling terms.

name: In τ language
summary: In tau-language, gluon self-interaction corresponds to non-commutativity of endomorphisms in the holonomy endomorphism algebra of the C-sector: the commutator [phi_a, phi_b] generates a third endomorphism describing gluon-gluon interaction.

name: Asymptotic freedom preview
summary: Gluon self-interaction is the structural origin of asymptotic freedom: the one-loop beta-function beta(g_s) is negative for N_f < 11N_c/2, so the effective strong coupling decreases at high energies and grows toward confinement at low energies.

name: Color Number Theorem
summary: The number of color charges is N_c = 3, uniquely determined by primorial depth d=3, the CRT decomposition Z/30Z = Z/2Z x Z/3Z x Z/5Z, removal of the polarity factor Z/2Z, and chi_minus-dominant resolution of the Z/3Z factor.

name: Asymptotic formula
summary: The strong coupling's (1-iota_tau) denominator is the structural signature of confinement: at high energy the effective coupling decreases toward the bare value iota_tau^3 (asymptotic freedom), while at low energy the denominator enhancement drives confinement.

name: Wilson Loop
summary: The Wilson loop W(gamma) = (1/3) Tr U(gamma) is the gauge-invariant trace of the holonomy around a closed path gamma, serving as the order parameter for confinement: area-law decay signals the confining phase.

name: Fractional CR-sublattice
summary: The fractional CR-sublattice Lambda_CR^{1/3} := {(m, n/3) : m,n in Z} subset Z x (1/3)Z refines the character lattice to accommodate color-charged modes with fractional eta-component n/3 not in Z.

name: Electric charge analogy
summary: The gamma-winding m in Z gives integer electric charges in units of e, while the eta-winding n gives color charge in thirds: quark charges +2/3 and -1/3 correspond to n equiv 2 and n equiv 1 mod 3; the asymmetry is the essence of confinement.

name: Color-confined mode
summary: A mode chi_{m,n} on T^2 is color-confined if n is not equiv 0 mod 3 (fractional eta-holonomy) and the associated boundary character fails to converge in H_partial[omega] as refinement stage N tends to infinity.

name: Confinement Theorem
summary: Confinement Theorem: no isolated color-charged state (eta-holonomy exp(2pi i c/3) with c not equiv 0 mod 3) resolves to a stable element of H_partial[omega]; the boundary character sequence fails to converge, so the state has no stable address on L.

name: Physical interpretation
summary: The Confinement Theorem says you cannot isolate a quark because there is no stable boundary address for it: the (1-iota_tau) denominator ensures the quark's address keeps refining forever, approaching a limit outside the admissible character space.

name: Comparison with the mass gap
summary: The Confinement Theorem addresses why quarks are confined; the complementary result -- why the strong sector has a mass gap (no massless gluon states) -- is derived via the tau-Gap Meta-Theorem in ch40.

name: Color singlet
summary: A composite state Psi is a color singlet if its total eta-holonomy is trivial: hol_eta(Psi) = 1, equivalently sum_j c_j equiv 0 mod 3, where c_j are the color charges of the constituents.

name: Singlet Stability
summary: A color singlet (sum c_j equiv 0 mod 3) resolves to a stable boundary character on L: the composite boundary character sequence converges in H_partial[omega] because the fractional eta-phases cancel exactly at each refinement stage.

name: Multi-quark states
summary: The singlet condition sum c_j equiv 0 mod 3 permits exotic composites beyond baryons and mesons: tetraquarks (qq bar{q} bar{q}) and pentaquarks (qqqq bar{q}) are admissible in principle and have been experimentally observed.

name: Singlet Classification
summary: Every persistent hadronic state is a color singlet; minimal singlet structures are baryons (three constituents {0,1,2} antisymmetric in color), mesons (quark-antiquark {c, bar{c}}), and exotic hadrons (k >= 4 constituents summing to 0 mod 3).

name: Linear Confinement Potential
summary: The defect functional D_C for a quark-antiquark pair with eta-separation delta satisfies D_C(delta) = D_C(0) + sigma_tau * delta + O(delta^2), where the tau-string tension sigma_tau = kappa(C;3) * g[omega]^2 establishes linear confinement.

name: Physical string tension
summary: The tau-string tension sigma_tau = kappa(C;3) * g[omega]^2 provides a structural formula for the QCD string tension; the coupling kappa(C;3) approx 0.0604 sets the scale, with the gap quantum g[omega] determined in ch40.

name: Baryon number
summary: Baryon number B(Psi) := (1/3) sum_j n_j is the total net eta-winding divided by 3, where n_j is the eta-winding of constituent psi_j; for a baryon with {c_1,c_2,c_3} = {0,1,2}, B = 1.

name: Winding Preservation
summary: Winding Preservation Lemma: any admissible endomorphism phi in HolEnd_tau compatible with the C-sector preserves the total eta-winding class modulo 3, ensuring baryon number conservation under all physically realizable processes.

name: Proton Stability
summary: Proton Stability Theorem: the proton is absolutely stable; no admissible endomorphism in the 4+1 sector framework can change baryon number, so B(phi(Psi)) = B(Psi) for all admissible phi, predicting tau_p = infinity.

name: Comparison with GUTs
summary: GUTs predict proton decay with tau_p >= 10^34 years via leptoquark bosons; Category tau predicts absolute stability because no admissible endomorphism mixes the eta-winding classes. Observed proton decay would falsify Category tau.

name: What about the electroweak sector?
summary: In the Standard Model, electroweak sphalerons violate B+L while preserving B-L; in Category tau, the sphaleron process is interpreted within the cross-sector coupling geometry rather than as baryon number violation.

name: The pattern of maturation
summary: The maturation pattern recurs throughout Book IV: 1st Edition geometric intuitions (Fourier duality, Higgs dismissal, parity assumption, topological confinement) are correct but insufficiently grounded, replaced by address obstruction, coherence fixing, and admissibility arguments.

name: The role of NF Discreteness
summary: NF Discreteness (III.P16) is the lynchpin of the gap theorem: it guarantees |C_n^{adm}| < infinity at each stage, converting infima into minima, so the minimum of a positive finite set is necessarily positive.

name: τ-Holonomy sector
summary: A tau-holonomy sector S consists at each finite stage n of a finite configuration space C_n (NF-enumerable), a finite admissible subset C_n^{adm} (polarity/boundary-coherent), a defect functional V_n to alpha-Idx, and surjective refinement projections.

name: Canonical vacuum at stage~n
summary: The canonical vacuum at stage n is Omega_n^* := argmin over C_n^{adm} of the lexicographic pair (V_n(Omega), code_n(Omega)), first minimizing the defect functional V_n, then tie-breaking by the smallest NF code.

name: Well-definedness
summary: For each n >= n_0, the canonical vacuum Omega_n^* exists (C_n^{adm} is finite and nonempty by III.P16), is unique (lexicographic order is total), and is computable (exhaustive search over a finite set).

name: Localized perturbations
summary: The localized perturbation set P_n(U) is the set of admissible perturbations p in C_n^{adm} supported within a region U subset T^2 such that the superposition Omega_n^* oplus p remains admissible.

name: Finite-difference quadratic form
